text.title: Traceability Auto-Maintenance — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that keep the traceability matrix synchronised with the tracker without giving the LLM write authority.

rule.statement: The tracker (Jira / Linear / GitHub) is the SOURCE OF TRUTH for ticket existence and titles. The matrix MIRRORS the tracker; never the reverse. If they disagree, the tracker wins and the matrix is reconciled.
rule.rationale: Two competing sources of truth produce two answers. The tracker is where engineers act; the matrix is where the BA reports. Aligning them on tracker-as-source eliminates the drift class.

rule.statement: Drift detector runs WEEKLY (default Monday 09:00). It compares every matrix link `(req_id → ticket_id)` to current tracker state and classifies into: VALID (ticket exists, title unchanged), RENAMED (exists, title changed), DELETED (ticket gone), MOVED (ticket migrated to new ID).
rule.rationale: Weekly cadence matches sprint rhythm. Daily is noise; monthly accumulates drift past human reconciliation capacity. Monday 09:00 surfaces drift before sprint planning.

rule.statement: LLM proposes REPAIRS as suggestions; it MUST NOT directly write to the matrix. Every repair suggestion lands in a queue + PR awaiting BA approval. Auto-merge is forbidden even for "obvious" repairs.

rationale.source: Requirements engineering integrity (INCOSE) / regulated-industry audit trails

rule.rationale: Auto-edited requirements artifacts lose audit trail and create compliance exposure (FDA, EASA, SOC-2). BA-in-the-loop is the only legally defensible pattern.

rule.statement: Every matrix link MUST declare a `link_type` from a closed list: IMPLEMENTS, VERIFIED-BY, DEPENDS-ON, REFINES, DERIVES-FROM. Repair suggestions preserve the original link_type — LLM may not "fix" by changing the link semantics.

rationale.source: ReqIF / IBM DOORS / OSLC RM standards

rule.rationale: Untyped links lose the audit story (test coverage vs implementation vs dependency). Repair that changes semantics quietly corrupts compliance reports.

rule.statement: Every drift detection AND every repair MUST emit an audit event with: timestamp, matrix path, tracker state hash, repair suggestions, BA approver, approval timestamp. Audit log persists for ≥ 7 years (FDA / EASA standard).

rationale.source: 21 CFR Part 11 / FDA software validation guidance

rule.rationale: Traceability matrices are evidence. Without an audit log of changes, the matrix's value to compliance reviewers collapses. 7 years matches FDA's record-retention rule.

example.title: Renamed ticket repair flow
example.code:
Matrix row: R-042 (Login MFA) → JIRA-2381 ("Implement MFA") link_type: IMPLEMENTS
Tracker state (week 14): JIRA-2381 → title now "Implement MFA + WebAuthn"
Detector verdict: RENAMED (same ID, title evolved) Repair suggestion: NONE NEEDED. Log title change in audit. Note in matrix: "title drift acknowledged 2026-W14".
example.note: Renamed is the common, harmless case. Detector logs it; no PR needed.

example.title: Deleted ticket repair flow
example.code:
Matrix row: R-067 (Audit export CSV) → JIRA-3104 link_type: VERIFIED-BY
Tracker state: JIRA-3104 → 404 (deleted by sprint cleanup)
Detector verdict: DELETED LLM suggestion: Search tracker for "audit", "export", "csv" tickets created in the last 60 days. Top candidate: JIRA-3210 ("Compliance report CSV export"), confidence 0.78.
Output: repair-suggestion PR with side-by-side diff. BA reviews: approves → PR merged → audit event logged.
example.note: r3 + r4: LLM suggests, BA approves, link_type unchanged.

example.title: Auto-merge repair (r3 violation)
example.code:
LLM detects DELETED → finds match with 0.78 confidence → auto-edits the matrix file. Commits to main without review.
2 sprints later: compliance review. Auditor asks "why does R-067 point to JIRA-3210?". No one knows. Audit log shows no human approval. Compliance finding raised.
example.note: Violates r3. Auto-merge destroys the audit chain.
